title: M&E Resident Technical Officer (RTO)
topic_label: materials equipment subcontractor, materials installations suppliers, equipment subcontractor
best_module: CE5809 – Management and Economics of International Construction
description: Qualification: A recognised Diploma in Mechanical/Electrical Engineering Building Services or ITC Certificate or equivalent. Experience: Minimum 10 years of relevant site experience in the supervision of building M&E Services. Familiar with local codes and regulations. Experience in new chilled water system A&A and new proposed development projects would be added advantage. Site Location: Loyang Way Expected Commencement Date: March 2026. Estimated Contract Period: 7 to 9 months. Working Days: 5.5 days per week. Duties and Responsibilities: The primary objective of the M&E RE/RTO shall be to assist the M&E Engineer/Client in ensuring that the M&E Installation works are in accordance with the specified requirements with respect to design, quality and performance. Duties and responsibilities shall among others, encompass the following: - General Assist the client/consultant to oversee all project activities; to prepare weekly progress report update to Clients/ Consultants. Prepare weekly progress report update to Clients/Consultants. To ensure that all installation methods and material used are in accordance with the contract drawings and specifications as well as client’s house rule and the Greenmark and Environmental Aspects and Impacts requirements. To liaise with the Contractors and the M&E Engineer in the checking and approval of shop drawings, catalogues and samples. To liaise with the Contractors in the co-ordination of site works. To liaise with the Contractors in resolving site problems. All problems on site shall be referred immediately to the M&E Engineer. The M&E RTO shall issue sketches or site memos as and when necessary. All site memos shall be copied to the M&E Engineer. The M&E RE/RTO shall not issue Instructions/Directions. To monitor and ensure that the site progress is in accordance with the approved construction programme. Any deviation from the construction schedule shall be reported immediately to the M&E Engineer. Technical To check on site that all materials used are of the approved type and of the best quality. To check on site that all installation works are of the approved methods as specified in the approved drawings. To check on site that all material and equipment used have been approved by the M&E Engineer. To ensure that the contractors record accurately on drawings, the installation works on site. This is to ensure that the ‘As-Built’ drawings reflect accurately the work that has been done on site. To check on site that all material and equipment installed on site are according to the code and client requirements approved by the M&E Engineer. Cost and Records To record, co-relate and file all incoming and outgoing design, shop, construction and co-ordination drawings. To record, co-relate and file all incoming and outgoing mail pertaining to the M&E works. To check and record the contractor's site progress. The M&E RE/RTO shall be required to submit the daily progress report to the M&E Engineer on a weekly basis. A monthly progress assessment/summary with comments on contractor's performance and forecast of manpower requirements and problem areas. To advise the M&E Engineer on the percentage of M&E works completed, equipment/material delivered to site for the purpose of progress payment evaluation. To check Contractors' claims and variation orders. Actual site check and measurement shall be carried out.

description: We are looking for a Geotechnical Design Engineer with experience in geotechnical design, analysis and writing technical proposals. Reporting to the Design Manager, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with the Business Development Team to provide design support for tenders. You will be expected to review tender specifications, requirements and draft the technical proposals. Likewise, you will be working closely with the Operations team to perform design-related coordination work, analysing geotechnical issues at project sites, and preparing detailed drawings for the site team. To be successful as a Geotechnical Design Engineer, you should be independent, demonstrate strong analytical thinking and be able to have prompt, open and direct communications with internal and external stakeholders to achieve project expectations. Outstanding candidates should be meticulous, detail oriented, demonstrate willingness to learn and display a positive attitude with a drive for results. Think you’ve got what it takes? A day in the life of Geotechnical Design Engineer Design support for tenders : Review and summarize soil condition reports, tender specifications and requirements. Identify and summarise geotechnical problems and technical risk. Analyse geotechnical problems and draft technical proposal. Design-related coordination work for ongoing projects : Perform analysis and design in accordance with current legislation, standards and codes of practice Prepare detailed construction drawings with the assistance of draughtsperson. Conduct regular site visits to integrate design concept and actual ground conditions. Quality Control (QC) review and verification between design assumptions and actual site conditions. Carry out geotechnical analysis and design using tools such as Slope/W and Plaxis as well as Keller’s in-house software. Conduct back analysis and research for future improvements based on actual performance and as-built data. Prepare technical proposal for remedial work during construction when required. Liaise with clients/consultants/ sub-contractors on technical related issues. Note: This role requires candidates to perform regular site walks at outdoor construction sites You will need: A professional degree in Civil or Geotechnical Engineering 1-3 years of relevant working experience in Geotechnical Engineering Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Powerpoint), AutoCAD, Plaxis 2D and Plaxis 3D A valid Supervise Construction Work for WSH certificate (preferable) What we offer: Annual Leave, Medical Insurance, Medical Leave, Variable Bonus, Learning & Development Opportunities, Referral Rewards & Employee Assistance Programme Global & Regional networking opportunities Friendly team and enjoyable working environment We encourage applications from the right candidates regardless of age, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, belief or race. description: Responsibilities: Manage a team of technical supervisors on site to ensure that all works comply fully with regulatory and client's requirements. title: Project Engineer
topic_label: construction activities, construction works, civil engineering
description:
Job Description & Requirements: We are looking for a dedicated Project Engineer to assist with the monitoring, scheduling, planning, resourcing, and managing of technical activities to ensure project accuracy and quality. The Project Engineer is responsible for the engineering and technical disciplines of ongoing projects and will establish inspection criteria for the proper
implementation of all the elements of a project. You should have the ability to work with multiple discipline projects and demonstrate excellent project management and supervision skills. You should also have excellent organizational, time management, leadership, and decision-making skills. The Project Engineer duties and responsibilities include but not limited to the following: - Project management, coordination, implementation, execution, control and completion of specific projects. Delegate tasks and responsibilities to appropriate personnel. Plan and schedule project timelines. Monitoring and follow up with the manufacturing and construction progress. Present reports defining project progress, problems and solutions. Facilitate site meeting with main contractor, consultant and clients. Ensure timely delivery and execution of projects. Provide engineering support during project tender phases. Any other duties that may be assigned by superior. JOB REQUIREMENTS Minimum a degree in Civil/Structural Engineering recognized by the respective Professional Bodies, professional institutions or BCA. Minimum 3 years working experience in building construction industry. Use of Autocad would be an advantage. Proficient in Microsoft Office. Good communicative skills, able to interact with people of all levels and teamwork.

title: Construction Manager (ARCH)
topic_label: construction activities, construction works, civil engineering
best_module: CE5808 – Digital Design and Construction (BIM and VDC)
description: The Construction Manager (Architectural & Façade Works) is responsible for leading the on-site execution, coordination, and delivery of architectural and façade projects, ensuring works are completed on time, within budget, and to the highest quality and safety standards. This role requires a technically competent leader who can manage site operations, supervise contractors and workers, coordinate with project managers and engineers, and ensure compliance with all relevant codes, safety regulations, and client specifications. The Construction Manager plays a key role in translating design intent into successful project execution. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Site Management & Execution - Plan, organize, and supervise daily site operations for architectural and façade works, ensuring adherence to project schedules and specifications. - Coordinate with project managers, engineers, and subcontractors to ensure work sequences and activities are efficiently executed. - Oversee installation of façade, cladding, glazing, and architectural finishes to meet design intent and quality standards. - Ensure timely availability of manpower, materials, and equipment to prevent delays. - Monitor site progress, identify potential issues, and implement corrective actions to maintain schedule compliance. 2. Quality Assurance & Technical Control - Enforce quality standards and workmanship requirements across all site activities. - Review shop drawings, technical submissions, and installation methods before work commencement. - Conduct inspections and quality checks to ensure materials and installations meet project and regulatory standards. - Coordinate mock-up reviews, sample approvals, and testing procedures with consultants and clients. - Maintain accurate site documentation, including inspection reports, NCRs, and QA/QC checklists. 3. Safety & Compliance - Ensure full compliance with Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) requirements and Environmental Management Systems (EMS). - Conduct regular safety briefings, toolbox meetings, and inspections to promote a safe working environment. - Investigate and report safety incidents, implementing corrective and preventive measures. - Enforce safe work practices among subcontractors and workers, ensuring adherence to method statements and risk assessments. 4. Resource & Subcontractor Management - Manage and coordinate subcontractors, ensuring alignment with project timelines, quality expectations, and cost objectives. - Evaluate subcontractor performance, progress claims, and variation works. - Liaise with procurement teams for timely delivery of materials and equipment. - Optimize use of site resources to minimize wastage and enhance productivity. 5. Coordination & Communication - Collaborate closely with the Project Manager, design team, and consultants to resolve technical or site-related issues. - Lead site coordination meetings and provide progress updates to management and stakeholders. - Facilitate clear communication between site teams, drafters, and fabrication workshops to ensure buildability and design alignment. - Support testing, commissioning, and handover processes. 6. Leadership & Continuous Improvement - Supervise and mentor site supervisors, foremen, and workers, ensuring competence and motivation within the team. - Promote a culture of quality, safety, and accountability at all levels of site operations. - Identify opportunities for construction innovation, efficiency, and continuous improvement. KEY COMPETENCIES · Strong leadership and communication skills with the ability to manage multi-disciplinary site teams. · In-depth knowledge of façade systems, architectural finishes, and installation methodologies. · Strong understanding of project scheduling, sequencing, and resource allocation. · Proven track record in delivering projects safely, on time, and with high-quality standards. · Excellent problem-solving and decision-making abilities in fast-paced environments. · Familiarity with local codes, safety regulations, and authority requirements. · Strong project management and organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. REQUIREMENTS · Diploma or Degree in Civil Engineering, Architecture, Building & Construction, or a related field. · Minimum 8–10 years of site management experience, including at least 5 years in architectural or façade works. · Proven experience managing large teams and subcontractors in complex projects. · Possess Safety for Project Managers Course (SPM) or equivalent certification. · Strong technical understanding of façade systems, materials, and installation techniques. · Excellent communication and coordination skills with both internal teams and external stakeholders. · Self-motivated, proactive, and able to work independently with minimal supervision. WORKING CONDITIONS - The standard working week is five and a half (5.5) days; however, you may be required to work on Saturdays and Sundays depending on project requirements. - Regular site presence is required to oversee installation and coordinate works.

Team Coordination: Manage, direct, and motivate construction workers, subcontractors, and other personnel to meet deadlines and work efficiently. Safety Oversight: Ensure that all construction activities follow health and safety regulations and that the site is a safe working environment. Quality Control: Monitor the quality of work, conduct inspections, and ensure compliance with project specifications and local building codes. Resource Management: Oversee the delivery, storage, and usage of materials, tools, and equipment on-site, ensuring optimal usage. Scheduling: Help develop and maintain construction schedules, ensuring projects stay on track and within deadlines. Problem Solving: Address and resolve issues on-site, including delays, material shortages, or unforeseen challenges. Communication: Serve as the primary point of contact for project managers, architects, engineers, subcontractors, and clients, ensuring effective communication and reporting. Documentation: Maintain daily reports on project progress, work completed, and any issues encountered. Document any incidents or safety violations. Compliance: Ensure all work is performed in compliance with legal and regulatory standards. Skills and Abilities: Strong leadership and supervisory skills to manage teams effectively. Excellent knowledge of construction processes, materials, and safety standards. Ability to read and interpret blueprints, plans, and technical drawings. Strong problem-solving and decision-making capabilities. Good organizational skills and attention to detail. Proficiency with construction management software (e.g., Procore, Buildertrend) is often preferred. Good communication skills to interact with various stakeholders. Job Requirements Education and Qualifications: High school diploma or equivalent (required). A degree or diploma in construction management, civil engineering, or a related field is preferred. Certification in construction safety (e.g., OSHA) is often required.

description: Job Description: Construction General Manager The Construction General Manager oversees and manages all aspects of construction projects, ensuring they are completed on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards. This role involves strategic planning, team leadership, financial management, and maintaining compliance with all regulations and safety standards. Key Responsibilities: Project Oversight: Plan, coordinate, and manage construction projects from inception to completion. Develop project timelines, schedules, and milestones to ensure deadlines are met. Oversee contractors, subcontractors, and laborers to ensure work meets quality and safety standards. Budget and Financial Management: Prepare and monitor project budgets, ensuring cost control and profitability. Approve expenditures, manage contracts, and negotiate with suppliers and vendors. Address and resolve budget discrepancies and financial challenges. Team Leadership: Supervise and lead project managers, site supervisors, and support staff. Recruit, train, and evaluate employees, fostering a productive and collaborative work environment. Resolve disputes and challenges between team members or stakeholders. Client and Stakeholder Relations: Serve as the primary point of contact for clients, ensuring expectations are understood and met. Prepare regular progress reports and communicate updates to stakeholders. Address client concerns and ensure satisfaction with project outcomes. Compliance and Safety: Ensure all work adheres to building codes, legal regulations, and safety standards. Conduct site inspections and enforce strict safety protocols. Manage permits, licenses, and documentation required for projects. Strategic Planning: Identify opportunities for business growth and expansion. Develop strategies for optimizing operational efficiency and reducing costs. Assess risks and implement contingency plans for unforeseen challenges.

title: construction supervisor

description: A Construction Supervisor oversees daily on-site operations, managing crews, subcontractors, and vendors to ensure projects are completed on time, within budget, and in compliance with safety regulations and building codes . title: CONSTRUCTION SUPERVISOR
topic_label: construction activities, construction works, civil engineering
description: Job Description & Requirements A Construction Supervisor with responsibilities for waterproofing, painting, and electrical duties plays a crucial role in overseeing and managing various aspects of a construction project. Here's a breakdown of the job scope for each area: Waterproofing (Open roof area) : Site Inspection: Conducting thorough inspections of the construction site to identify areas that require waterproofing. This includes assessing the condition of surfaces, joints, potential water entry points, and PU Grouting. Material Selection: Choosing appropriate waterproofing materials based on site conditions, local building codes, and project requirements. Supervision of Application: Overseeing the application of waterproofing materials, ensuring proper techniques are followed to create a seamless and effective barrier against water intrusion. Quality Control: Conducting regular quality checks to ensure that waterproofing work meets industry standards and project specifications. Painting: Surface Preparation: Ensuring that surfaces to be painted are properly cleaned, repaired, and primed before applying paint. This may involve tasks like sanding, filling cracks, and applying sealants. Color and Material Selection: Assisting in the selection of appropriate paint types, colors, and finishes based on project requirements, design plans, and client preferences. Supervision of Application: Overseeing the painting process to ensure proper techniques are used, including correct mixing of paint, application methods, and drying times. Quality Assurance: Conducting inspections to verify that the painting work meets quality standards and adheres to project specifications. Electrical Duties: Installation Oversight: Supervising the installation of electrical systems, including wiring, outlets, switches, lighting fixtures, and other electrical components according to approved plans and local building codes. Coordination with Electricians: Working closely with electricians to ensure that electrical work is carried out safely and in compliance with industry standards. Testing and Commissioning: Conducting tests to ensure proper functioning of electrical systems and coordinating with relevant parties for any necessary adjustments or corrections. Safety Compliance: Ensuring that all electrical work is performed in a safe manner, with proper grounding, insulation, and protection measures in place. General Responsibilities: Team Management: Supervising a team of workers, assigning tasks, and providing guidance and support to ensure that work is carried out efficiently and to the required quality standards. Communication: Maintaining clear and effective communication with other stakeholders, including project managers, architects, subcontractors, and clients. Documentation: Keeping accurate records of work progress, materials used, and any incidents or issues that arise during the construction process. In addition to these specific duties, a Construction Supervisor in this role will also be responsible for ensuring compliance with safety regulations, managing schedules, and coordinating with other trades to ensure a smooth and successful construction process. They may also be involved in budget management, procurement of materials, and problem-solving as issues arise on-site. On-site will majorily be outdoors under the sun. Minimum of 3 years experience required.

title: Foreman
topic_label: construction activities, construction works, civil engineering
description: Job Description & Requirements Position Summary: A Construction Foreman is responsible for overseeing the day-to-day operations of a construction site, ensuring that all work is completed on time, within budget, and in compliance with safety regulations. They supervise and lead the construction crew, coordinate activities, and ensure that the project is executed according to plans and specifications. The Foreman also acts as the point of contact between the site workers and higher-level management. Key Responsibilities: Team Leadership & Supervision: Supervise, direct, and manage construction workers, ensuring that tasks are completed on schedule and to specification. Provide clear instructions to workers, delegate tasks, and maintain productivity on-site. Train new workers and provide ongoing guidance to ensure proper work methods and safety standards are followed. Project Coordination: Collaborate with the site manager, project manager, and other team members to ensure efficient daily site operations. Monitor and ensure that the construction work adheres to the project’s specifications, timeline, and budget. Coordinate with subcontractors, suppliers, and other stakeholders to ensure materials and equipment are delivered on time. Safety Compliance: Enforce safety rules and regulations on the job site to prevent accidents and injuries. Conduct regular safety inspections and implement corrective measures as needed. Ensure that all workers are using proper personal protective equipment (PPE) and are aware of site safety protocols. Quality Control: Inspect all aspects of the construction process to ensure the work meets the project specifications and quality standards. Identify and address any issues, errors, or defects, ensuring that they are corrected before proceeding. Work with the project manager to address any quality concerns and ensure compliance with building codes. Material and Equipment Management: Ensure that materials, tools, and equipment are available on-site and properly stored. Monitor inventory levels, report material needs, and ensure the correct materials are used according to the plans. Supervise the proper use and maintenance of construction equipment and machinery. Schedule and Productivity Management: Ensure that tasks are completed according to the project timeline and work schedules. Track progress, identify delays, and take corrective actions as necessary to maintain the schedule. Coordinate with other trades and workers to ensure that activities are carried out smoothly and efficiently. Documentation and Reporting: Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of daily work activities, materials used, workforce attendance, and safety checks. Report any significant issues, delays, or changes to the project manager or construction manager. Document incidents or accidents on-site and ensure proper reporting protocols are followed. Problem-Solving & Issue Resolution: Address any on-site issues related to work quality, delays, safety concerns, or material shortages. Provide solutions for any challenges that arise during the construction process, ensuring that work can continue without disruption. Skills and Qualifications: Experience: Minimum of 3 years in a construction foreman or similar supervisory role. Technical Knowledge: Strong understanding of construction methods, blueprints, materials, and building codes. Leadership Skills: Ability to lead, motivate, and manage a diverse team of workers. Problem-Solving: Quick decision-making abilities to resolve issues that may arise during the construction process. Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to coordinate with workers, subcontractors, and management. Time Management: Ability to organize work tasks efficiently, manage time well, and meet deadlines. Attention to Detail: High level of attention to ensure work meets quality and safety standards. Physical Requirements: Ability to work in various weather conditions, standing or walking for long hours. Ability to lift heavy objects and engage in physical tasks as required on-site. Ability to perform inspections of the construction site and identify potential safety hazards or issues with the work.

Key Responsibilities: Team Leadership & Supervision: Supervise, direct, and manage construction workers, ensuring that tasks are completed on schedule and to specification. Provide clear instructions to workers, delegate tasks, and maintain productivity on-site. Train new workers and provide ongoing guidance to ensure proper work methods and safety standards are followed. Project Coordination: Collaborate with the site manager, project manager, and other team members to ensure efficient daily site operations. Monitor and ensure that the construction work adheres to the project’s specifications, timeline, and budget. Coordinate with subcontractors, suppliers, and other stakeholders to ensure materials and equipment are delivered on time. Safety Compliance: Enforce safety rules and regulations on the job site to prevent accidents and injuries. Conduct regular safety inspections and implement corrective measures as needed. Ensure that all workers are using proper personal protective equipment (PPE) and are aware of site safety protocols. Quality Control: Inspect all aspects of the construction process to ensure the work meets the project specifications and quality standards. Identify and address any issues, errors, or defects, ensuring that they are corrected before proceeding. Work with the project manager to address any quality concerns and ensure compliance with building codes. Material and Equipment Management: Ensure that materials, tools, and equipment are available on-site and properly stored. Monitor inventory levels, report material needs, and ensure the correct materials are used according to the plans. Supervise the proper use and maintenance of construction equipment and machinery. Schedule and Productivity Management: Ensure that tasks are completed according to the project timeline and work schedules. Track progress, identify delays, and take corrective actions as necessary to maintain the schedule. Coordinate with other trades and workers to ensure that activities are carried out smoothly and efficiently. Documentation and Reporting: Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of daily work activities, materials used, workforce attendance, and safety checks. Report any significant issues, delays, or changes to the project manager or construction manager. Document incidents or accidents on-site and ensure proper reporting protocols are followed. Problem-Solving & Issue Resolution: Address any on-site issues related to work quality, delays, safety concerns, or material shortages. Provide solutions for any challenges that arise during the construction process, ensuring that work can continue without disruption. Skills and Qualifications: Experience: Minimum of 3 years in a construction foreman or similar supervisory role. Technical Knowledge: Strong understanding of construction methods, blueprints, materials, and building codes. Leadership Skills: Ability to lead, motivate, and manage a diverse team of workers. Problem-Solving: Quick decision-making abilities to resolve issues that may arise during the construction process. Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to coordinate with workers, subcontractors, and management. Time Management: Ability to organize work tasks efficiently, manage time well, and meet deadlines. Attention to Detail: High level of attention to ensure work meets quality and safety standards. Physical Requirements: Ability to work in various weather conditions, standing or walking for long hours. Ability to lift heavy objects and engage in physical tasks as required on-site. Ability to perform inspections of the construction site and identify potential safety hazards or issues with the work.

description: T&C Engineer - Electrification Projects Navsar Global is a premier specialist services firm, connecting top-tier talent with leading companies in critical infrastructure sectors such as power generation, data centres, and oil & gas. We are currently seeking a highly skilled and dedicated T&C Engineer to join our client's dynamic team, focusing on impactful Electrification Projects. The Role: As a T&C Engineer, you will be a pivotal member of the project team, coordinating daily testing and commissioning workflows on-site. You will take full ownership of all testing activities for complex Electrification Projects, ensuring meticulous adherence to defined standards, customer requirements, economic objectives, quality benchmarks, schedule timelines, and stringent Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) protocols. Key Responsibilities: Collaborate effectively with internal teams, various departments, clients, and system-wide contractors and subcontractors to arrange and execute site activities seamlessly. Actively participate in critical meetings such as Works Train Notice Meetings, Operator / LTA Track work Access meetings, and LTA manpower planning meetings (e.g., for C853e and HGBD works). Prepare and submit necessary documentation, including SDAR (Site Daily Activity Report) and TAR (Track Access Request), to secure track access and ensure testing proceeds on schedule. Lead and manage commissioning activities across the entire lifecycle, from Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT), Pre-Site Acceptance Testing (PRE-SAT), Site Acceptance Testing (SAT), System Integration Testing (SIT), through to final handover to the client and successful service commencement. Support the Project Manager in the preparation of essential project documentation, including Method Statements, Risk Assessments, detailed step-by-step work plans, and other required paperwork. Develop and implement robust systems and platforms for monitoring project progress on a weekly basis, providing timely feedback to the project planner as assigned. Supervise and provide hands-on support to installation teams for SKD RT A&A (Installation activities) at the site, fostering a self-motivated and efficient working environment. Assist the Manager in preparing comprehensive work schedule programs. Undertake ad-hoc duties assigned by the Manager that are critical to the project's success. Demonstrate a strong commitment to teamwork, ensuring smooth project completion through collaborative effort. Be willing to work extended hours, including night shifts (engineering hours) and public holidays, as project demands necessitate. Exhibit strong decision-making capabilities in fast-paced project environments. Provide support to other project teams as and when required to achieve overall project objectives. Qualifications: Education & Training: High School Diploma or equivalent. Diploma in Electrical & Electronic or Mechanical Engineering. Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Electrical/Electronic Engineering is highly preferred. Experience: Minimum of 3 years of hands-on working experience in Electrification projects. Proven track record of taking responsibility and ownership of assigned tasks and outcomes. Demonstrated experience in using requirements traceability. Knowledge of Siemens electrification solutions is a significant advantage. Experience in reviewing and updating drawings, specifications, Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) plans, FAT reports, and Site Acceptance Test (SAT) reports. Hands-on experience with high voltage and secondary voltage test equipment in T&C works. Strong understanding of safety significance in the operation and maintenance of High, Medium, and Low Voltage systems, as well as Traction Power Systems in a railway environment, with safety as the highest priority. Proven ability to take complete responsibility for Site Acceptance Testing (SAT/ITC/TTR). Hands-on experience or knowledge of PLC systems (e.g., Siemens, Mitsubishi). Knowledge in ETTS/TSSS systems. Technical Competencies: Basic knowledge of competitor systems in the market. Basic understanding of Functional Safety (IEC 61508) fundamentals and safety functions within core components. Proficiency in Global Business English, both speaking and writing, to facilitate clear international communication. Strong communication skills, including active listening, clarifying, interpreting, and adapting to various situations and personalities. Basic knowledge and application of virtual communication and collaboration tools. Advanced understanding of internal and external laws and regulations to ensure ethically proper business acumen; ability to advise colleagues on compliance-related issues. Basic knowledge of contract formats, practices, and policies, understanding when and whom to involve for assistance. Basic understanding and application of information security practices to protect data and systems. Basic knowledge of regional labor laws and regulations, applying them to daily work and knowing escalation procedures. Basic knowledge of the relevant product, system, and service portfolio within the area of responsibility, including ability to suggest process improvements and solve simple technical problems. Basic technical product and system knowledge relevant to the organization, understanding technical differences and utilizing documentation effectively.

description: Job Summary: The Electrical Project Engineer will play a crucial role in the planning, design, and execution of electrical and ELV systems for industrial/commercial construction projects in Singapore. This includes overseeing the installation, commissioning, and maintenance of electrical systems, ensuring they meet both safety standards and regulatory requirements. The ideal candidate will have experience working on construction projects, collaborating with contractors, architects, and other engineers to deliver high-quality electrical solutions on time and within budget. Key Responsibilities: 1. Electrical Design and Planning: Develop detailed electrical design plans for the construction project, including power distribution, lighting systems, electrical control systems, and ELV systems. Collaborate with architects, mechanical engineers, and other specialists to integrate electrical systems seamlessly into the overall building design. Conduct site surveys and coordinate with other teams to collect necessary data for design. 2. Project Management: Assist in creating and maintaining the project schedule for electrical works, ensuring key milestones are met and deadlines adhered to. Support the Project Manager in managing project budgets, estimating electrical materials, labor, and other resources required. To perform material take-off to prepare BQ (Bill of Quantities). Ensure that all electrical installations are carried out as per approved designs, on time, and within the allocated budget. Coordinate procurement of electrical equipment, ensuring compliance with project specifications and timelines. 3. Installation, Testing, and Commissioning: Supervise and coordinate the electrical installation, ensuring all work is performed in accordance with drawings and specifications. Oversee the testing and commissioning of electrical systems to ensure proper functionality and compliance with safety standards. title: Senior Construction Operations Manager - Founding Member, New Strategic Business Unit
topic_label: singapore construction industry, singapore construction, civil structural engineer

description: An Exceptional Opportunity to Shape the Future of a Growing Medium-Sized Enterprise's Greenfield Construction Venture in Singapore The Company: A successful and growing medium-sized enterprise with a strong track record in related industries is strategically establishing a new construction business unit in Singapore to capitalize on significant market opportunities. This is a unique and high-impact opportunity to be a foundational member of this venture, leveraging the established reputation and agility of a thriving company while building a dynamic and focused operation from the ground up. We are seeking an exceptional and entrepreneurial Senior Construction Operations Manager to spearhead site operations, drive business development, and establish robust regulatory and HR frameworks. The Role: As the Senior Construction Operations Manager, you will be a pivotal figure in the success of this new strategic business unit. This multifaceted role demands a seasoned construction professional with a proven track record in managing complex projects, a strong business acumen for client acquisition, and a deep understanding of Singapore's regulatory landscape, particularly within the construction sector. You will be instrumental in setting the operational blueprint, securing initial projects, and building a high-performing team. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Leadership & Operational Blueprint: Collaborate with executive leadership to define the operational strategy and establish core procedures, workflows, and performance standards for the new construction business unit. Lead the identification, evaluation, and acquisition of essential operational resources, including initial equipment, technology platforms, and strategic supplier partnerships, aligned with the company's growth       strategy. Define and champion the company's approach to project execution excellence, stringent quality assurance protocols, and industry-leading safety management systems. Strategic Business Development & Key Client Acquisition: Spearhead business development initiatives by leveraging your extensive network and proactively identifying and engaging with key potential clients, including developers, architects, and other businesses. Contribute strategically to the development of compelling business development materials, presentations, and proposals that align with the company's brand and value proposition. Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with strategic clients, industry influencers, and potential partners to secure initial and future projects. Represent the company at relevant industry events, conferences, and networking forums to build brand presence and generate leads. Provide critical operational and costing expertise in the preparation of complex bids and tenders, ensuring competitive and profitable proposals. Establish robust tracking and reporting mechanisms for business development activities and  outcomes. Project Leadership & Elite Execution (Flagship Projects): Directly lead and oversee the execution of the company's initial, high-profile construction projects, ensuring delivery on time, within stringent budgetary parameters, and to high-quality standards that reflect the company's commitment to excellence. Build, mentor, and empower a team of capable project managers, supervisors, and skilled workers, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Serve as the primary point of operational leadership, reporting directly to the designated General Manager or Business Unit Head on all strategic and tactical matters related to site and project performance. Chair comprehensive project briefings and strategic kick-off meetings, setting clear expectations and ensuring alignment across all stakeholders. Develop and present concise, data-driven periodic progress reports for internal executive review and external client communication. Strategic Construction Planning & Resource Optimization: Provide strategic direction in establishing advanced construction planning methodologies and resource allocation strategies for the new business unit. Establish sophisticated and efficient coordination protocols between internal project teams, pre-qualified subcontractors, key clients, and external consultants, ensuring seamless project delivery aligned with master schedules. Contribute to the development of standardized, company-level cost estimation and control processes, overseeing progress claim reviews, interim certifications, and payment authorizations. Strategically forecast and secure all necessary resources (highly skilled manpower, appropriate equipment fleets, suitable machinery, and quality materials) from project inception to completion, with a strong emphasis on optimized resource utilization and adherence to the company's financial guidelines. Enterprise-Level Risk Management & Regulatory Compliance: Proactively identify and mitigate potential risks across initial projects, translating lessons learned into robust company-wide risk management frameworks and best practices. Ensure unwavering compliance with all Singaporean regulatory requirements, including stringent quality, safety, and environmental standards, establishing a culture of compliance that aligns with the company's ethical and operational standards. Oversee the submission and clearance processes with relevant Singaporean authorities, establishing efficient and compliant protocols for all future projects. Implement and enforce adherence to approved specifications and relevant industry standards,       embedding a commitment to quality assurance throughout the organization. Establish, implement, and continuously monitor a comprehensive safety management system, conducting rigorous periodic safety audits on all initial sites to set the benchmark for a safety-first culture. Strategic HR & Regulatory Framework Development: Leverage a deep understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations to strategically plan and manage foreign worker quotas, work permit applications, and other critical HR practices within the construction sector, aligning with the company's HR policies. Collaborate with the company's HR function to establish the business unit's HR policies, procedures, and talent acquisition strategies, ensuring compliance with local regulations and the company's overall standards. Utilize your comprehensive knowledge of Singapore's business certification and licensing requirements to ensure the new construction firm operates with full legal compliance from its inception, working with relevant internal teams or external advisors. Lead the application and acquisition of all essential business licenses and permits, ensuring a smooth and timely setup process. Strategically plan and manage initial project staffing to optimize workforce deployment while adhering strictly to MOM regulations and quota limitations. Strategic Ad-hoc & Foundational Initiatives: Undertake strategic ad-hoc assignments directed by executive leadership, the Construction Manager, and the Business Unit Head, particularly those critical to establishing the new company's operational, business development, and regulatory frameworks in alignment with the company's overall strategy. Job Requirements: Bachelor's Degree in Construction Management, Engineering (Civil, Structural, or equivalent), or a related field from a reputable institution. Advanced degree preferred. Equivalent to 15+ years of progressive and impactful experience within the construction sector in Singapore, with significant and demonstrable success as a Senior Construction Site Manager or equivalent management role on complex, large-scale projects. Exceptional and comprehensive knowledge of best-in-class project management methodologies, tools, and technologies across all project lifecycle stages, with a strategic understanding of the unique challenges and opportunities of a new business unit within a growing enterprise. Proven and demonstrable track record of successfully soliciting new business, building strong client relationships, and contributing to significant business development wins within the Singaporean construction market. Deep and current understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations concerning foreign worker quotas, work permit processes, and strategic HR management within the construction industry. Comprehensive knowledge of the necessary business certifications, licenses, and regulatory requirements for operating a construction firm in Singapore, including interactions with relevant government agencies. Mastery of MS Project and the MS Office suite, with proficiency in other relevant project management and collaboration tools. A strategic and passionate leader with exceptional people management skills, demonstrated ability to build and motivate high-performing teams in a start-up or new business unit environment, and a commitment to fostering a culture of excellence and innovation. A results-oriented and decisive individual with exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and negotiation skills, crucial for navigating the complexities of a new business and the Singaporean regulatory landscape. World-class written, communication, and interpersonal skills, essential for engaging with executive leadership, diverse stakeholders, building strategic client relationships, securing new business, and effectively interacting with regulatory bodies. Unwavering willingness to work flexibly, including extended hours as required during critical project phases, business development initiatives, and the strategic establishment of the new business unit. A proven ability to thrive under pressure, manage complex priorities, and deliver exceptional results within demanding timelines. A strategic, proactive, and highly entrepreneurial mindset with a strong personal drive to build and shape a significant new construction business unit within a growing enterprise, with a direct and measurable impact on its growth and success across operations, business development, and regulatory compliance. Working Conditions: A dynamic and challenging leadership role based in Singapore, requiring a commitment of 5 working days per week, with the understanding that significant flexibility and extended hours will be necessary, particularly during the      initial strategic phases of the business unit's establishment, critical flagship project milestones, and strategic business development activities. This is a rare and compelling opportunity to leave a lasting legacy by building a significant new construction business unit within a thriving medium-sized enterprise. If you possess the strategic vision, deep industry expertise, entrepreneurial spirit, and proven leadership capabilities to excel in this foundational role, we encourage you to submit your confidential application.

title: Research Fellow

description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. Develop distributed and scalable inference frameworks and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Co-supervise graduate students and research assistants. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in token communication, Physical AI, and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We’re Looking For A Doctoral degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline, with a specialization on wireless communication and distributed machine learning A strong background in communication theory, signal processing, and optimization. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and MATLAB. A proven track record of publishing research papers in esteemed conferences or journals in wireless communication and AI. Strong communication skills and the capability to work effectively in a collaborative research environment.

Provides constructive critics of TA Event activities after completion; contribute towards Lesson Learnt/After Action Reviews lookback processes. Working with site SME to develop skill sets for equipment unique to the individual site. Carry out any other related duties as required, including temporary assignments in other sections within the MTAC department on site. You are what we are looking for if you: Have Degree/Diploma or equivalent in Electrical Engineering Have minimum 8-10 years relevant working experience in electrical construction/maintenance Are an Authorized Electrical Person (AEP) in M site Have completed HV Operation Course by EMA Have LEW qualiﬁcation preferably Have Oil/petrochemical industry safety course for supervisors.

title: INSTSLLAR
topic_label: electrical engineer, electrical works, commissioning electrical
description: Construction Electrician Duties and Responsibilities Construction Electricians play a vital role in building projects, as they install and maintain the electrical systems that power the structures. They must understand blueprints, follow local building codes, and ensure safety is a priority at all times. A construction electrician refers to one whose profession is to install, maintain, and repair electrical power, communications, lighting, and control systems on industrial, residential, and/or commercial buildings. In essence, a construction electrician is responsible for installing and repairing complex electrical control, wiring, and lighting systems working from a technical blueprint.Their daily duties and responsibilities include: Reading and interpreting electrical and construction blueprints, diagrams and specifications Planning layout and installation of electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures, based on job specifications and local codes Connecting wires to circuit breakers, transformers, or other components Testing electrical systems and continuity of circuits in electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures to ensure compatibility and safety of system Inspecting electrical systems, equipment, and components to identify hazards, defects, and the need for adjustment or repair, and to ensure compliance with codes Installing, maintaining, modifying and repairing all electrical distribution systems and associated electrical equipment Utilizing a range of tools and equipment, such as power construction equipment, measuring devices, power tools, and testing equipment Training and mentoring apprentice electricians, providing them with the skills and knowledge needed in the field Ordering electrical supplies and equipment. Reviewing blueprints or technical diagrams Overseeing the installation and maintenance of wiring, control, and lighting systems Carrying out inspections on electrical components, such as transformers and circuit breakers Training and directing workers to install, maintain, and/or repair electrical wiring equipment Installing electrical apparatus, equipment, and fixtures for systems such as alarm Troubleshooting electrical issues with the appropriate utilization of relevant testing devices Complying strictly with National Electrical Code state and local building regulations Carrying out plans for electrical wiring for well-functioning lighting, intercom, and other electrical systems.

title: AI Engineer
topic_label: ai capabilities, ai, ai solutions
description: JOB OVERVIEW: As an AI Engineer, you will leverage cutting-edge AI to solve complex, industry-specific problems, particularly within the maritime sector. You will be instrumental in a rapidly evolving, client-centric organization, making a significant impact by thinking critically, articulating a compelling vision, and guiding both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Your role requires a proven ability to architect complex AI/ML solutions from problem framing to production-ready design, a profound understanding of diverse business models, and strong consultative sales skills to identify strategic pain points solvable by AI. You will build functional AI/ML prototypes with clean, modular code for production, assess data readiness, identify data gaps, and recommend robust data governance frameworks. Additionally, you will identify emerging trends, synthesize complex information, and present strategic recommendations to senior leadership and external audiences. Success hinges on your ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business value, build strong client relationships, and drive consensus across diverse teams. You must be highly motivated to drive initiatives independently in a dynamic startup environment, with a passion for continuous learning and innovation. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: •	Proactively identify and engage with high-value business opportunities and potential clients who can significantly benefit from advanced AI and machine learning solutions. Deeply understand client business challenges, pain points, and strategic goals, translating them into clear, solvable AI/ML problems. •	Design end-to-end AI solutions, articulating compelling value propositions and the strategic roadmap for adoption. •	Conduct in-depth assessments of client data infrastructure, data governance, data quality, and overall data maturity. •	Make expert judgment calls on data "AI-readiness" and provide actionable recommendations for data improvement, gap identification, and strategic data capitalization opportunities. •	Rapidly develop functional AI prototypes and Proof-of-Concepts (POCs) that are architecturally sound, adhere to best practices for maintainability, and are designed for production. •	Continuously research and evaluate the latest AI advancements, including Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), Deep Learning, and MLOps methodologies. Synthesize findings into actionable insights, influencing our company's strategic roadmap, service offering development, and contributing to industry thought leadership through presentations to leadership and external forums (moderate to high emphasis). •	Collaborate effectively with the internal AI Production team, ensuring prototypes and solution designs are well-documented, understood, and seamlessly integrated into the full development lifecycle. •	Clearly define the business case, expected return on investment (ROI), and measurable benefits of proposed AI/ML implementations, simplifying complex technical concepts for non-technical audiences. QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCES: •	Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field. Master's or Ph.D. preferred. •	At least 5+ years of progressive experience in AI/ML solution design, data science, or AI engineering, with a significant portion in a client-facing or consulting capacity. •	Proven experience in designing, developing, and deploying functional AI/ML prototypes that demonstrate business value and are engineered for production. •	Expert proficiency in Python and extensive experience with core AI/ML libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch). •	Demonstrated hands-on experience with Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs), including frameworks like LangChain, for designing and prototyping novel applications. •	Strong practical experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) services relevant to AI/ML (e.g., Vertex AI, BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow, Cloud Functions). •	Proficiency with Docker for containerization and a foundational understanding of container orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes concepts for GKE). •	Solid understanding of MLOps principles for reproducibility, experiment tracking (e.g., MLfl ow, Vertex AI ML Metadata/Model Registry), and model versioning. •	High-level expertise in data governance frameworks, data quality assessment (including tools/libraries like Great Expectations), metadata management (e.g., Google Cloud Data Catalog), and data architecture principles. •	Strong expertise in distributed computing and parallel processing techniques is vital for handling large-scale AI workloads. •	A proven track record of innovations and executions in deep learning, demonstrated through shipping products or first-author publications at leading AI conferences, is a strong differentiator. •	Experience in the container shipping industry or related logistics/supply chain domains is highly advantageous. •	Excellent presentation, documentation, and stakeholder management skills. Working hours: Mon to Fri, 8:30am to 5:30pm

description: ABOUT THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF EDUCATION (NIE) The National Institute of Education (NIE), Singapore , is Singapore’s national teacher education institute and we are proud to be an integral part of the nation’s education service. We play a key role in the preparation of teachers and in the provision of teacher professional and school leadership development programmes. We are committed to our vision of being An Institute of Distinction: Leading the Future of Education and our mission to Inspire Learning, Transform Teaching and Advance Research . Read more about NIE here . NIE invites applications for the position of part-time software developer for the project “PhET-style simulations to promote AI literacy”. The project’s goal is to develop a curated library of interactive, high-quality online resources for teachers that puts NIE at the forefront of AI literacy. To work towards this goal, the project will start out by developing customizable web-based simulations and corresponding avatar-based narrative instructional videos catering to three fundamental topics in AI literacy - Personalization using Machine Learning and Neural Networks , Ethical Issues in AIED , Human-AI Collaboration. These simulations would allow student teachers to experiment with relevant critical task features in a hands-on manner, thus improving their understanding of abstract technical concepts in AI and the implications of their usage in AI systems for education. Project evaluation would focus on usability evaluation and learning evaluation of the programmed simulations. Facilitation resources and validated design guidelines for developing effective AI literacy simulations will be made available to educational stakeholders upon project completion. This is a contract position till 30 November 2026. Responsibilities Gathering requirements for simulation design from domain experts Agile prototyping of mock UI/UX interfaces for the simulations Programming and iteratively refining the simulations based on expert feedback and usability testing Building data logging capabilities in the designed simulations Developing facilitation guides to assist stakeholders’ work with designed simulations Working with NIE’s IT teams to host simulations securely and ensure seamless interoperability with existing NIE platforms Managing the project and ensuring timely completion of milestones Requirements Essential A Polytechnic Diploma Full stack software development and debugging experience Knowledge of computer graphics and interface design Fluent in coding with generative AI Solid understanding of fundamental machine learning and AI techniques Creativity, good communication skills and ability to work as team player Able to work independently with minimal supervision Preferred Work, teaching, or corporate training experience in AI for education Familiarity with AI literacy efforts worldwide, in APAC and Singapore Up-to-date with latest advancements in generative AI (for education) Passion for improving teaching and learning with technology Application We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. description: Job Overview We are seeking an experienced satellite Engineer with at least three years of professional hands-on experience in satellite design, development, and deployment. This role involves both technical development and educational outreach, ensuring participants gain practical skills in satellite design, assembly, and operation. Key Responsibilities Training Program Development and Delivery •	Design and deliver comprehensive training programs on satellite building, covering topics such as: o	Systems engineering o	Subsystems (power, communication, attitude control, etc.) o	Payload integration o	Satellite testing and deployment protocols •	Develop instructional materials, including manuals, presentations, and hands-on projects, to suit various audience levels (youth, professionals, and educators). •	Conduct hands-on workshops and labs, guiding participants through real-world satellite assembly and testing activities. •	Collaborate with academic institutions, industry partners, and space agencies to expand training opportunities and resources. •	Other Topics outside of Satellite Engineering that would be good to cover: o	Space mission design and planning o	Principles of Space Engineering and Applications o	Project Management in Space Evaluation and Continuous Improvement •	Evaluate training program outcomes through feedback, assessments, and participant performance. •	Continuously update training content to reflect advances in satellite technology and industry trends. •	Provide mentorship to participants during and after training programs to support their career or project goals. Qualifications and Skills Required •	Bachelor’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. •	Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience in satellite development, including design, integration, and testing. •	Strong understanding of satellite subsystems, including power systems, communications, payload integration, and attitude determination and control. •	Proficiency with satellite design tools (e.g., STK, MATLAB, SolidWorks, or similar). •	Experience with satellite testing equipment and procedures, including vibration testing, thermal testing, and deployment mechanisms. •	Excellent communication and teaching skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts for diverse audiences. •	Experienced in conducting training programmes for youths, undergraduates and professionals •	Experiences: Satellite Engineering •	Apply expertise in designing, building, and testing satellites, including CubeSats and other small satellite platforms. •	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure training programs align with the latest developments in satellite technology and industry standards. •	Maintain and operate testing facilities, including cleanrooms, thermal vacuum chambers, and other satellite testing equipment. Preferred •	Master’s degree or certification in satellite technology or systems engineering. •	Experience in delivering STEM or technical training programs. •	Familiarity with satellite mission operations and ground station integration. •	Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, C++, or MATLAB for satellite systems. •	Experience working with space agencies or commercial satellite companies. Key Attributes •	Passionate about inspiring and educating others in satellite technology. •	Innovative and adaptable in designing hands-on learning experiences. •	Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail in both technical and instructional tasks. •	Team-oriented, with the ability to collaborate across departments and with external stakeholders. ________________________________________ Benefits •	Opportunity to shape the next generation of satellite engineers and innovators. •	Competitive salary and benefits package. •	Professional development opportunities in both technical and educational fields.

title: Physical AI Engineer
topic_label: ai capabilities, ai, ai solutions
description:
We are looking for Physical AI Engineers focused on developing and
integrating Machine Learning (including Generative AI) models that enable real robots to perceive, reason, and act autonomously. This role bridges Robotics and Agentic AI — working on perception, decision-making, and multi-model integration for multi-robot and embodied systems. You will work on training and adapting Generative/Foundation models
(vision, language, planning, VLA models, swarm reasoning) and deploying them onto edge and robotic hardware. Responsibilities ·       Develop and integrate AI/ML and Generative/Foundation models for perception, mapping, task planning, and embodied decision-making. ·       Fine-tune or adapt Generative/Foundation models (Small Language Model/Small-Vision-Language/ Vision-Language-Action) models for edge deployment for real-world robotic applications. ·       Implement on-device model optimization (quantization, distillation, TensorRT, etc.). ·       Build data pipelines: simulation → real-world → feedback loop for continual model improvement. ·       Conduct domain adaptation and reinforcement/interactive learning for robot skills. ·       Integrating models into ROS2 and swarm autonomy stacks. ·       Evaluate model performance in both simulation and hardware deployment. ·       Optimize inference latency and reliability on edge compute platforms. ·       Formulate the conceptual and detailed technical solution for the development of applications to meet customer requirements. ·       Provide recommendations on relevant emerging technology in Physical AI/Robotics to senior management. ·       Identifying and leading strategic technical capability development for Physical AI/Robotics. ·       Collaborate on research and development projects to explore new capabilities and applications for Physical AI/Robotics technology. Minimum Requirements ·       Bachelor’s/Master’s in Computer Science, Machine Learning, AI, Robotics, or related field. ·       3 years of experience building robotics/Physical AI systems. Candidates without work experience but with relevant skills are also welcome to apply. ·       Strong foundation in ROS2 or robotics middleware integration. ·       Extensive experience building/fine-tuning of AI/ML and Generative/Foundation models (vision, transformer-based, or RL) for robotic systems. ·       Good knowledge of model compression/acceleration for embedded devices (Jetson, Pi,etc). ·       GPU/CUDA experience or on-device inference for embedded AI. ·       Experienced with deployment on embedded/edge Linux systems. ·       Good understanding of robotics model training pipelines (perception → planning →control). Preferred Experience ·       Vision-Language-Action (VLA) or Multi-Modal model experience. ·       Reinforcement learning / imitation learning / interactive training loops. ·       Synthetic data and simulation-based training (Isaac Sim, Habitat, etc.). ·       Knowledge of distributed training pipelines or MLOps for robotics. Additional Skills ·       Strong experimentation and iterative problem-solving mindset. ·       Comfortable bridging research prototypes into production-grade systems. ·       Able to collaborate tightly with AI and systems engineers. ·       Curious and self-driven — able to explore new Physical AI approaches and rapidly test them. What We Offer ·       Opportunity to work on state-of-the-art embodied AI models powering real robots. ·       Combination of research and deployment — not just writing models, but seeing them act in the physical world. ·       High-impact work on cutting-edge robotic autonomy and swarm behaviours.

Role Overview Location: Singapore Type: Full-time Job Description We are looking for a Frontend Engineer to build and scale Ropedia’s user-facing products, including our data platform web application , company website , and mobile app experiences . You will work closely with product, design, and backend/ML teams to deliver high-quality, performant, and reliable interfaces , turning complex workflows into intuitive interactions for customers and internal operators. In this role, you will own key frontend architecture and implementation, drive UI engineering best practices, and help shape the overall product experience across web and mobile. Responsibilities 1.      Design and implement frontend experiences for Ropedia’s data platform , including core workflows, dashboards, and interactive tools. 2.     Build and maintain Ropedia’s marketing website and product pages with strong attention to visual quality, responsiveness, and performance. 3.    Develop and iterate on mobile app user experiences (e.g., capture/operations tooling, user-facing product flows), ensuring consistency
across platforms. 4.    Collaborate closely with design and product to translate Figma specs into production-quality UI, and continuously improve interaction quality. 5.    Work with backend/infra teams to define and implement API contracts ,

title: Founding Research Scientist
topic_label: ai capabilities, ai, ai solutions
description:
About Us Ropedia is a Singapore-based, early-stage AI startup focused on building the data infrastructure for spatial and physical intelligence. Our core team brings together expertise from Tsinghua, Meta, Google, and UC Berkeley, and we have already achieved early-stage commercial viability and backed by top-tier VC and angel investors from Google, Nvidia, Amazon and etc. We develop proprietary wearable and mobile capture devices and models to collect, reconstruct, and perceive multimodal human Xperience in real-world scenarios. Our mission is to power the next generation of World Models and Embodied AI. We offer compensation competitive with top-tier tech giants, along with significant early-team equity and incentives. Role Overview Location: Singapore Type: Full-time Job Description You will join the founding research team
and lead model design and research in core areas such as world models, 3D/4D
perception, and egocentric understanding. Multiple research tracks are open
under this role, and candidates may match one or more directions based on their
background. Responsibilities ·     Design and implement models for world and scene understanding using egocentric, multi-view, and multimodal data. ·     Work closely with engineering and hardware teams to deliver reusable, maintainable research code and internal tools. ·     Co-design data collection protocols, evaluation metrics, and ablation studies. ·     Produce research papers, internal technical reports, and demo systems at appropriate stages of the project. Requirements ·     PhD (or equivalent research experience) in Computer Vision, Machine Learning, Graphics, Robotics, or related fields. ·     Strong track record in at least one of the following areas: human modeling, 3D/4D reconstruction, egocentric vision, or scene/world modeling. ·     Proficient in Python and mainstream deep learning frameworks (PyTorch preferred). ·     Solid foundation in 3D geometry, camera models, and numerical optimization. ·     Able to drive ideas from concept to implementation and deployment. Clear communication skills are required. Research Directions A. Human Modeling & Motion Understanding Focus: Human/hand/face modeling, motion and deformation priors, human–object interaction, and affordance modeling. Preferred Background: Experience in human pose/shape estimation, SMPL-type models, motion capture, or motion generation. B. 3D/4D Reconstruction & Neural Rendering Focus: Multi-view and dynamic scene reconstruction, NeRF/Gaussian Splatting, novel view synthesis. Preferred Background: Experience in 3D reconstruction or neural
rendering; familiarity with camera calibration and bundle adjustment (BA). C. Egocentric Vision & Interaction Focus: Egocentric action and intention understanding, hand–object interaction, gaze/attention modeling, and task structure modeling. Preferred Background: Experience in video understanding, action recognition, or egocentric vision. D. Scene Reconstruction & World Modeling Focus: Long-term consistent 3D/4D scene mapping, scene graphs, object- and space-centric representations, and spatial reasoning. Preferred Background: Experience in large-scale mapping, semantic reconstruction, or agent world models.

title: Natural Language Processing Research Fellow (Thai Language Specialist) for AI Singapore (Products)
topic_label: ai capabilities, ai, ai solutions
description: AI Singapore (AISG) is a national AI programme launched by the National Research Foundation (NRF) to anchor deep national capabilities in Artificial Intelligence (AI). The programme office is hosted by the Nanyang Technological University and brings together all Singapore-based research institutions and the vibrant ecosystem of AI start-ups and companies developing AI products to perform use-inspired research, grow the knowledge, create the tools, and develop the talent to power Singapore's AI efforts. We are seeking a highly motivated and talented Research Fellow to join our team and contribute to cutting-edge research in multilingual Large Language Models (LLMs) and Natural Language Processing (NLP), with a specific focus on Southeast Asian languages. This is an exciting opportunity to work in a dynamic and rapidly evolving field, pushing the boundaries of language technology and making a real impact on under-represented languages. description:

comparison and variance analysis. Ability to work with numbers, spreadsheets, and cost data
accurately. Good communication skills to coordinate with project, procurement,
and finance teams. Familiarity with ERP systems (e.g. Workday, SAP, Navision) and
Excel is essential. Organised, systematic, and able to manage multiple cost items and
projects concurrently.

Our team works directly with world-class AV partners to ensure this technology is deployed safely, seamlessly, and efficiently, meeting the highest industry and regulatory standards for Southeast Asia. To achieve this, we are building a deep, in-house expertise in vehicle integration, testing, and hardware operations. We are a senior, hands-on engineering group that prizes operational excellence, clear interfaces, and reproducible pipelines. Get to Know the Role As a Senior AV Test Engineer, you are the crucial technical bridge between our AV partners' technology and Grab's real-world operations. Your mission is to oversee the vehicle platform, lead our hands-on testing efforts, and ensure that the integrated system performs safely and reliably on Southeast Asia's roads. You will report to the Head of Engineering and work onsite in Grab One North, Singapore office. The Critical Tasks You Will Perform Be the primary owner for the on-vehicle AV hardware suite provided by our partners, including drive-by-wire systems, sensors, and compute (ECUs). You will be responsible for system bring-up, maintenance, and diagnosing hardware-level issues. Lead the execution of rigorous test plans on proving grounds and in the field, running manoeuvre tests (like lane changes and emergency braking), failure mode checks and documenting results to validate the performance of the AV system. Develop the on-vehicle testing and diagnostics infrastructure, writing scripts and tools for logging, monitoring, and debugging complex hardware-software interactions in real-time. Work with our partners and safety teams to validate system performance, reproduce issues, and ensure the vehicle platform meets all functional and safety requirements. Support data operations for the testing fleet, managing data ingestion, upload to the cloud, and asset tracking to support validation and analysis. Experience with testing, integration, or systems engineering role for autonomous vehicles (AV), ADAS, or complex robotics systems. Hands-on experience with automotive hardware, including ECUs and networking protocols (e.g., CAN, Automotive Ethernet). Experience with vehicle dynamics and actuation systems (braking, steering, throttle). Familiarity with automotive safety standards and their impact on integration. Experience with on-vehicle debugging, diagnostics, and logging systems (e.g. flashing ECUs, debugging hardware/software interactions). Debugging and problem-solving skills in real-world, noisy environments. A big plus if you have: Proficiency in Python, for scripting, diagnostics, and tooling. Experience with Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) and Software-in-the-Loop (SIL) testing methodologies. Experience with real-time operating systems (e.g., QNX, Linux RT).
